title: How to lower a assert from firrtl.assert to verif.assert
body:
Sorry if I am missing something, but I can't seem to find a way to export a system verilog file with a normal assert statement.

## input
Chisel input
```scala
assert(cond)
```
## expected
```verilog
always @(posedge clock)
    assert(cond);
```
or
```verilog
always @(posedge clock)
    assert(~reset && cond);
```
## actual
```verilog
if (~reset & cond) begin
  if (`ASSERT_VERBOSE_COND_)
    $error("Assertion failed\n    at ... assert(cond)\n");
  if (`STOP_COND_)
    $fatal;
end
```


Looking at some of the code it seems that only failing or sv assertions output is supported.

Is there any way to output a normal assert statement?
